content:
The man who built Tenor, sold it to Google, and watched Google kill it is now running the company Google is funding to replace it. This isn’t irony. It’s a business model.

5 min read
Just now

--

On June 30, 2026, Google shut down the Tenor API. No extension. No exceptions. Every third-party integration Discord, WhatsApp, Bluesky, and thousands of smaller apps started returning errors the same day.
Most users never knew Tenor existed. They just noticed their GIF picker stopped working and blamed the app. GIFs shared on Discord or Twitter were quietly sourced through the API users encountered Tenor without knowing it, and their frustration has largely been directed at the platforms rather than Google.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Ai Generated ImageThat’s the first layer. The second layer is weirder.
The Timeline Is Worth Reading Slowly
TENOR: FROM ACQUISITION TO SHUTDOWN
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2018 Google acquires Tenor to power GIF search
across Google Images, Gboard, Messages2023 Cost cuts begin. Free API with no clear
revenue model becomes an easy target.
Jan 13 '26 Google announces API deprecation…
